rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED AL-QARAWI, BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD VS. DOMINICAN UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2013, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANTs BERLITZ LANGUAGES, INC., ELS EDUCATIONAL SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendants' demurrer to the Amended Cross Complaint ("ACC") is overruled as to the first, second, third and fifth causes of action and sustained as to the fourth cause of action. As to the first cause of action for statutory indemnity, Cross-Complainant has sufficiently pled it was an agent of Cross-Defendants (ACC 12), and that it provided corporate services to Cross-Defendants in good faith and in their best interests. (ACC 13). As to the second and third causes of action for implied and equitable indemnity, the demurrer is overruled. Each alleges facts sufficient to state a cause of action. As to the fourth cause of action for contribution, the demurrer is sustained because Cross-Complainant has not pled that a money judgment was rendered jointly against two or more defendants. (See General Electric Co. v. State of CA (1973) 32 Cal. App. 3rd 918, 925.) Cross-Defendants demurrer to the fifth cause of action is overruled; Cross-Complainant pleads an actual controversy between the parties. The special demurrer for uncertainty is overruled. Cross-Defendants can determine what claims are directed against them. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal. App. 4th 612, 616.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 24, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answer To Post-Judgment Discovery Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Case Title: JANNICKE KOLDERUP et al VS. OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant acted with a conscious disregard to plaintiff's safety. The evidence demonstrates that Mr. Craig decided to cap the second flue without ever consulting with plaintiff or investigating whether the flue was still in operation. (See Defendants' UMF 31-34; Plaintiffs' UMF 18 (Kolderup Declaration 16.) The Court rejects defendant's argument that plaintiff has no evidence that defendant's director, officer, or managing agent engaged in oppressive conduct or ratified such conduct. This issue was raised for the first time in the reply brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Frances Sussna'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Palo Alto Medical Foundation, Gregory Engle, M.D., Dr. Conrad Massimo Vial, And Dr. Graham Beaumont And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS TO PALO ALTO MEDICAL FOUNDATION, GREGORY ENGLE, M.D., DR. CONRAD MASSIMO VIAL, and DR. GRAHAM BEAUMONT AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. Granted, in part. The subpoena to Palo Alto Medical Foundation (PAMF) shall be quashed, but may be modified and reissued pursuant to a court order. The subpoena to PAMF, Plaintiff's general purpose healthcare provider, is overbroad in seeking "any and all" records without restriction as to type of medical condition, scope or time. The subpoena to PAMF shall be limited to cardiac (heart) treatment (including heart diagnosis, treatment and procedures), obesity, shortness of breath, blood pressure and neurological issues. The motion to quash subpoenas directed specifically to Dr. Vial and Dr. Engle, Plaintiff's cardiac surgeon and cardiologist, respectively, is denied. Plaintiff has placed her heart condition and heart surgery at issue. The parties are to meet and confer and attempt to agree regarding the time limitation, if any, to be placed on these subpoenas. As to the subpoena to Dr. Graham Beaumont, if Plaintiff can clarify and stipulate that: (1) she does not claim severe emotional distress over and above that which would ordinarily be associated with an injury of this type; and (2) at trial she will claim no economic damages for therapy bills and will not present expert testimony by any mental health provider at trial, then the Court will quash the subpoena to Dr. Graham Beaumont (psychiatrist). If she does not so clarify, a hearing will be required on the subpoena to Dr. Beaumont. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CURATOLO VS. JANE GRONA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike, Or In Tha Alternative Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY CURATOLO's Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, Tax Costs. The motion to strike or in the alternative tax costs is denied in its entirety. Defendant's costs memo is timely under CRC 3.1700 and Fries v. Rite Aid Corp. (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 182. The deposition costs were reasonable and defendant properly substantiates them. (Bertrand dec., Ex. 4). The Court further notes that plaintiff failed to provide a copy of the costs memo or object to the costs in compliance with CRC 3.1700(b)(2). The court denies plaintiff's attempt to tax $310 because that issue was raised for the first time in the reply brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. The motion is granted. Plaintiff Anne-Marie Yantes is hereby dismissed from this action with prejudice and is ordered to pay sanctions to Defendant in the amount of $318. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MAIQUI EFREN BRITO et al VS. SEKOU BANKS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT SEKOU BANKS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike the punitive damage prayer in the complaint is denied. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support a claim for exemplary damages. "One who willfully consumes alcoholic beverages to the point of intoxication, knowing that he thereafter must operate a motor vehicle, thereby combining sharply impaired physical and mental faculties with a vehicle capable of great force and speed, reasonably may be held to exhibit a conscious disregard of the safety of others." Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 897. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
